questions.question: Framework/approach for /mcp OAuth
questions.rationale: Purpose-built for 'I have Google creds, hand MCP clients tokens'; ships PRM + AS metadata + DCR shim + PKCE; reuses our Google app; no external billing. Cost is the SDK->standalone fastmcp migration (mostly imports).

questions.question: Bridge: OAuth-user -> Connection mapping
questions.recommendation: A: OAuth establishes a per-user 'Mode A' Connection
questions.rationale: A Connection is the unit of a playing session (pause/concurrency/stall/dashboard). Mapping OAuth -> per-user connection keeps one unified model and leaves require_connection/play API/connector untouched. Lowest risk, coherent long-term.

questions.question: /mcp back-compat for X-Connection-Key header
questions.rationale: Operator chose OAuth-only at /mcp; it is also the simpler implementation since the FastMCP OAuth provider gates all /mcp traffic by default. Raw sk_conn_ HTTP API stays as the non-/mcp fallback.

questions.question: Client scope for v1 'done'
questions.recommendation: Claude required; Codex/Gemini best-effort (operator chose all four)
questions.rationale: Operator requires all four clients to pass before shipping. Risk: Codex/Gemini MCP-OAuth maturity is outside our control with no /mcp header fallback; verify each client early, escalate via block if a client cannot do spec OAuth.

assumptions: We reuse the existing Google OAuth app/credentials (client_id/secret) already used for human login; the MCP OAuth proxy is an additional consumer of the same Google app, with its own redirect URIs registered.
Each client's current MCP-OAuth support is verified early in the plan stage; a client that cannot do spec OAuth is escalated via block, not worked around in code.
discovery.summary: Replace the X-Connection-Key paste step at /mcp with one-click Google OAuth via fastmcp v3 GoogleProvider (OAuthProxy). Bridge = OAuth establishes a per-user 'Mode A' Connection (Option A); require_connection/play API/connector untouched. /mcp becomes OAuth-only; raw sk_conn_ HTTP API remains the non-/mcp fallback. All four clients (Claude Code/Desktop, Codex, Gemini CLI) must pass before shipping.

answers.Framework/approach for /mcp OAuth: fastmcp v3 GoogleProvider (OAuthProxy); migrate from SDK-bundled FastMCP to standalone fastmcp; reuse existing Google client_id/secret; we own a JWT signing key.
answers.Bridge: OAuth-user -> Connection mapping: A: OAuth establishes a real per-user 'Mode A' Connection. /mcp doorway resolves the Google token -> find-or-create that user's canonical connection -> forward its sk_conn_ key to the internal API. require_connection + play API + connector UNCHANGED. Treated as a durable connection (pause/resume/concurrency/dashboard apply). HTTP-loopback-with-minted-key is an isolated impl detail that can later be refactored to an in-process call independently.
answers./mcp back-compat for X-Connection-Key he: OAuth-only at /mcp. Drop the X-Connection-Key header path AT /mcp; /mcp clients must connect via OAuth. The raw sk_conn_ HTTP API (connector) still works as the non-/mcp fallback. Note: this is also the simpler implementation (FastMCP OAuth provider gates all /mcp traffic by default). The CLAUDE-constraint 'play API accepts BOTH' refers to the internal HTTP API, not /mcp — no conflict.
answers.Client scope for v1 'done': All four (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Codex, Gemini CLI) must pass real end-to-end OAuth connect before shipping. RISK: Codex/Gemini MCP-OAuth maturity is outside our control and there is no header fallback at /mcp; if a client genuinely cannot do spec OAuth (DCR/PKCE discovery) it is a block-to-human decision, not an implementation fix. Mitigation: verify each client's MCP-OAuth support EARLY (plan stage), per the 'validate against real clients early' constraint.

non_goals: Changing the connector's auth (it keeps using raw sk_conn_).
Removing the sk_conn_ / require_connection auth path on the internal HTTP API.
Refactoring require_connection to accept OAuth identity directly (Option B was rejected).
MCP-driven new-user onboarding/agent creation: a user with zero agents who completes OAuth simply gets a 'waiting/no agent' state and still creates agents on the web.
The in-process loopback refactor: v1 may call the internal HTTP API with the minted key; replacing that with an in-process service call is deferred.
acceptance_criteria: A player connects each of Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Codex, and Gemini CLI to /mcp via a one-click Google OAuth flow with NO pasted key, no secret in URL, no key in chat, and plays a full turn end-to-end.

An unauthenticated request to /mcp returns a spec-compliant 401 with WWW-Authenticate pointing at the RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata; the AS metadata, DCR shim, and PKCE flow complete and the client retries with a Bearer token successfully.
After OAuth, the user's MCP tool calls act for ANY of that user's agents (resolved by Agent.user_id) via a single per-user 'Mode A' connection that the /mcp layer finds-or-creates.
The connector (raw sk_conn_ on the HTTP API) and the existing require_connection play API continue to work unchanged.
Preflight Gate is green: ruff + mypy app/ mcp_server/ + pytest, with no suppressions.

checklist.goal: Replace the 'paste sk_conn_ key as X-Connection-Key header' MCP connect step with a true one-click, secure OAuth flow (Google-backed) so players connect their AI client to /mcp with no long-lived secret to copy, no key in URL, no key in chat.
checklist.audience: Players connecting an AI client (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Codex, Gemini CLI; NOT Cursor) to /mcp for Mode A interactive play.
checklist.constraints: MCP auth-spec compliance (RFC 9728 PRM + 401 challenge + AS metadata + DCR shim + PKCE); Google has no DCR so a proxy/bridge is mandatory; play API must accept BOTH OAuth and raw sk_conn_ (connector keeps using sk_conn_, never touched /mcp); no key in URL, avoid key-in-chat, tokens narrow+rotatable; repo standards (no suppressions, type annotations, async, Preflight green); validate against REAL clients (Claude Code + Claude Desktop) early, not at the end.
